maml:description.maml:para: Disables Windows Recovery Environment.
Disables the Windows Recovery Environment. Requires -Force or explicit confirmation because the operation is high-impact.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables WinRE without prompting and returns the new state.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a folder mount point from a recovery partition.
Removes the specified folder mount point from a previously mounted recovery partition.
Folder mount path (for example C:\Mounts\Recovery) used as an access path for the recovery partition.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Folder mount path (for example C:\Mounts\Recovery) used as an access path for the recovery partition.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Dismounts the recovery partition mount point at the specified folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables Windows Recovery Environment.
Enables the Windows Recovery Environment and returns the resulting configuration.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables WinRE and returns the new state.

maml:description.maml:para: Discovers recovery partitions on local disks.

Enumerates physical disks and emits a RecoveryPartitionInfo object for each partition that carries the Windows recovery partition type GUID. Use -IncludeNonRecovery to widen the search to non-recovery partitions for diagnostics.
Optional disk filter. When omitted, every physical disk is searched.
When set, the discovery surface also returns partitions that do not look like recovery partitions; useful for diagnostics.
Optional partition filter applied to the results of the disk-level enumeration.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Scopes discovery to a set of disks: CurrentOSDisk (default; the disk hosting the running OS), AllDisks, or SecondaryDisksOnly (every disk except the OS disk). Prevents recovery/BCD operations from fanning out across every disk on dual-disk or dual-boot systems. Ignored when -DiskNumber is supplied.
Optional disk filter. When omitted, every physical disk is searched.
When set, the discovery surface also returns partitions that do not look like recovery partitions; useful for diagnostics.
Optional partition filter applied to the results of the disk-level enumeration.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Scopes discovery to a set of disks: CurrentOSDisk (default; the disk hosting the running OS), AllDisks, or SecondaryDisksOnly (every disk except the OS disk). Prevents recovery/BCD operations from fanning out across every disk on dual-disk or dual-boot systems. Ignored when -DiskNumber is supplied.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists every recovery partition on disk 0.
Discovers recovery partitions on disk 0 using a splatted OrderedDictionary parameter set.

maml:description.maml:para: Discovers recovery boot entries.
Enumerates Boot Configuration Data entries and returns those that look like recovery boot entries. Use -IncludeAll to return every entry regardless of recovery heuristics.
Path to a boot WIM image used by the BCD recovery entry.
When set, returns every BCD entry instead of only those that match recovery heuristics.
When set, returns boot entries that are flagged as hidden in BCD.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Path to a boot WIM image used by the BCD recovery entry.
When set, returns every BCD entry instead of only those that match recovery heuristics.
When set, returns boot entries that are flagged as hidden in BCD.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists the recovery boot entries whose name starts with "Grace".

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the current Windows Recovery Environment configuration.
Queries the active Windows Recovery Environment configuration and returns a structured WindowsRecoveryEnvironmentInfo object.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns the current WinRE configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Discovers Windows RE or Windows PE image files.
Enumerates WIM files in the supplied path and returns structured information about each candidate recovery image.
When set, computes a SHA-256 hash of each discovered image. Slower but useful for change detection.
Image classification (WindowsRE, WindowsPE, BootWim) used when tagging the result.
File-system path to search for WIM images.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill Source Description }}

Scopes discovery to a set of disks: CurrentOSDisk (default; the disk hosting the running OS), AllDisks, or SecondaryDisksOnly (every disk except the OS disk). Prevents recovery/BCD operations from fanning out across every disk on dual-disk or dual-boot systems. Ignored when -DiskNumber is supplied.
When set, computes a SHA-256 hash of each discovered image. Slower but useful for change detection.
Image classification (WindowsRE, WindowsPE, BootWim) used when tagging the result.
File-system path to search for WIM images.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill Source Description }}

Scopes discovery to a set of disks: CurrentOSDisk (default; the disk hosting the running OS), AllDisks, or SecondaryDisksOnly (every disk except the OS disk). Prevents recovery/BCD operations from fanning out across every disk on dual-disk or dual-boot systems. Ignored when -DiskNumber is supplied.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists WIM files in the specified folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Mounts a recovery partition at a folder mount point.
Assigns a folder mount point so the recovery partition can be inspected from Explorer or other tooling.
Folder mount path (for example C:\Mounts\Recovery) used as an access path for the recovery partition.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Folder mount path (for example C:\Mounts\Recovery) used as an access path for the recovery partition.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Mounts the recovery partition on disk 0 partition 5 at the specified folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a recovery partition on a target disk.

Creates and formats a recovery partition on the specified disk. Use -SizeBytes for explicit sizing or -SizePercent for percentage-based sizing (mutually exclusive parameter sets). The partition is always placed after the existing partitions; -CreationMode controls how trailing free space is obtained. Image and boot-entry payloads are staged by the dedicated cmdlets (Set-WindowsRecoveryImage, New-WindowsRecoveryBootEntry).
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
File system used to format the recovery partition. Defaults to NTFS.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
File system label assigned to the recovery volume. Defaults to RECOVERY.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Explicit partition size in bytes. Mutually exclusive with -SizePercent.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Placement strategy. The recovery partition is always created after the existing partitions (never before the OS). UseTrailingFreeSpace (default) appends into existing free space at the end of the disk and never moves or resizes anything, failing if there is not enough room. ShrinkToFit shrinks the last partition (typically the OS) by the shortfall to free trailing space first. RequireEmptyDisk only creates on a disk with no partitions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
File system used to format the recovery partition. Defaults to NTFS.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
File system label assigned to the recovery volume. Defaults to RECOVERY.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Partition size expressed as a percentage of the target disk size. Mutually exclusive with -SizeBytes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Placement strategy. The recovery partition is always created after the existing partitions (never before the OS). UseTrailingFreeSpace (default) appends into existing free space at the end of the disk and never moves or resizes anything, failing if there is not enough room. ShrinkToFit shrinks the last partition (typically the OS) by the shortfall to free trailing space first. RequireEmptyDisk only creates on a disk with no partitions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
File system used to format the recovery partition. Defaults to NTFS.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
File system label assigned to the recovery volume. Defaults to RECOVERY.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Explicit partition size in bytes. Mutually exclusive with -SizePercent.
Partition size expressed as a percentage of the target disk size. Mutually exclusive with -SizeBytes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Placement strategy. The recovery partition is always created after the existing partitions (never before the OS). UseTrailingFreeSpace (default) appends into existing free space at the end of the disk and never moves or resizes anything, failing if there is not enough room. ShrinkToFit shrinks the last partition (typically the OS) by the shortfall to free trailing space first. RequireEmptyDisk only creates on a disk with no partitions.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a 1 GiB recovery partition on disk 0 using existing trailing free space.
Creates a percentage-sized recovery partition, shrinking the last partition to make room if the disk has no trailing free space.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a custom recovery boot entry from a boot image.

Stages a boot image onto a destination and creates a matching BCD boot entry. The destination is a recovery partition (written through its \?\GLOBALROOT path with no mount), an already-mounted directory (-TargetPath), or the image's existing location (the default ByImagePath set). By default the WIM is staged as-is and booted as a ramdisk, and a boot.sdi is resolved automatically (or supplied with -BootSdiPath); with -ExpandBootImage the image is expanded flat onto the partition root (Microsoft flat-boot layout, systemroot=\Windows) and the entry is wired to boot it in place. For ramdisk boot the staging sub-path defaults to \Recovery\WindowsRE but is overridable via -StagingRelativePath. Existing entries with the same name are not duplicated.
When set, appends the new BCD entry to the end of the boot order instead of the default position.
Whether the recovery BCD boot entry is Visible or Hidden in the boot menu.
Path to a boot WIM image used by the BCD recovery entry.
Boot menu timeout applied when a new recovery boot entry is configured.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
When set, marks the new boot entry as the default in BCD.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Explicit boot.sdi to stage for ramdisk boot. When omitted it is resolved from the live OS and then extracted from the boot image.

Expands the boot image flat onto the destination (non-RAM / flat boot) and wires the entry to boot it in place, instead of staging the WIM for ramdisk boot. Requires a destination (-RecoveryPartition or -TargetPath).
One-based image index inside the WIM to expand when -ExpandBootImage is used. Defaults to 1.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Recovery partition (RecoveryPartitionInfo, typically piped from Get-RecoveryPartition) to stage the boot image onto and target with the BCD entry. Files are written through the partition's \?\GLOBALROOT path without assigning a drive letter or mounting the volume.
Volume-relative folder the image (and boot.sdi) are staged into. Defaults to \Recovery\WindowsRE; pass an empty string or a single backslash () to stage at the volume root.

Formats the target recovery partition (NTFS, quick) before a flat expansion so the image lands on a clean volume with no overlapping content. Only honoured with -ExpandBootImage on a -RecoveryPartition. DESTROYS all existing content on that partition, including any existing \Recovery\WindowsRE payload.
When set, appends the new BCD entry to the end of the boot order instead of the default position.
Whether the recovery BCD boot entry is Visible or Hidden in the boot menu.
Path to a boot WIM image used by the BCD recovery entry.
Boot menu timeout applied when a new recovery boot entry is configured.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
When set, marks the new boot entry as the default in BCD.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Explicit boot.sdi to stage for ramdisk boot. When omitted it is resolved from the live OS and then extracted from the boot image.

Expands the boot image flat onto the destination (non-RAM / flat boot) and wires the entry to boot it in place, instead of staging the WIM for ramdisk boot. Requires a destination (-RecoveryPartition or -TargetPath).
One-based image index inside the WIM to expand when -ExpandBootImage is used. Defaults to 1.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Volume-relative folder the image (and boot.sdi) are staged into. Defaults to \Recovery\WindowsRE; pass an empty string or a single backslash () to stage at the volume root.
An already-mounted directory to stage the boot image into. Use this when the destination volume has a drive letter or mount point.

Formats the target recovery partition (NTFS, quick) before a flat expansion so the image lands on a clean volume with no overlapping content. Only honoured with -ExpandBootImage on a -RecoveryPartition. DESTROYS all existing content on that partition, including any existing \Recovery\WindowsRE payload.
When set, appends the new BCD entry to the end of the boot order instead of the default position.
Whether the recovery BCD boot entry is Visible or Hidden in the boot menu.
Path to a boot WIM image used by the BCD recovery entry.
Boot menu timeout applied when a new recovery boot entry is configured.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
When set, marks the new boot entry as the default in BCD.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Explicit boot.sdi to stage for ramdisk boot. When omitted it is resolved from the live OS and then extracted from the boot image.

Expands the boot image flat onto the destination (non-RAM / flat boot) and wires the entry to boot it in place, instead of staging the WIM for ramdisk boot. Requires a destination (-RecoveryPartition or -TargetPath).
One-based image index inside the WIM to expand when -ExpandBootImage is used. Defaults to 1.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Recovery partition (RecoveryPartitionInfo, typically piped from Get-RecoveryPartition) to stage the boot image onto and target with the BCD entry. Files are written through the partition's \?\GLOBALROOT path without assigning a drive letter or mounting the volume.
Volume-relative folder the image (and boot.sdi) are staged into. Defaults to \Recovery\WindowsRE; pass an empty string or a single backslash () to stage at the volume root.
An already-mounted directory to stage the boot image into. Use this when the destination volume has a drive letter or mount point.

Formats the target recovery partition (NTFS, quick) before a flat expansion so the image lands on a clean volume with no overlapping content. Only honoured with -ExpandBootImage on a -RecoveryPartition. DESTROYS all existing content on that partition, including any existing \Recovery\WindowsRE payload.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stages winre.wim onto the recovery partition and creates a ramdisk recovery boot entry.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a recovery partition.

Removes a recovery partition. Requires -Force or explicit confirmation because the operation is destructive. The cmdlet inspects the surrounding partition layout and refuses to remove when the immediately following partition is the OS partition unless -Force is supplied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the recovery partition on disk 0 partition 5 without prompting.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a recovery boot entry idempotently.

Removes a BCD boot entry by identifier, name, or pipeline input. -Name accepts wildcards and removes every matching entry. With -DeleteImageFiles the staged WIM and boot.sdi the entry booted from are also deleted off the recovery partition. Requires -Force or explicit confirmation because the operation is destructive.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
BCD GUID identifier of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Also deletes the staged WIM and boot.sdi that the entry booted from off the recovery partition (best effort). Opt-in because file deletion is irreversible; by default only the BCD objects are removed and the staged files are left in place.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Object received from the pipeline that the cmdlet should act on.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Also deletes the staged WIM and boot.sdi that the entry booted from off the recovery partition (best effort). Opt-in because file deletion is irreversible; by default only the BCD objects are removed and the staged files are left in place.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Also deletes the staged WIM and boot.sdi that the entry booted from off the recovery partition (best effort). Opt-in because file deletion is irreversible; by default only the BCD objects are removed and the staged files are left in place.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
BCD GUID identifier of the target boot entry.
Object received from the pipeline that the cmdlet should act on.
Friendly display name of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Also deletes the staged WIM and boot.sdi that the entry booted from off the recovery partition (best effort). Opt-in because file deletion is irreversible; by default only the BCD objects are removed and the staged files are left in place.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes every recovery boot entry whose name starts with "Grace" and deletes their staged image files.

maml:description.maml:para: Resizes a recovery partition to the requested size.

Resizes a recovery partition. Use -SizeBytes for explicit sizing or -SizePercent for percentage-based sizing. The two are mutually exclusive parameter sets. The cmdlet inspects the surrounding partition layout and refuses to grow when there is not enough trailing free space unless -Force is supplied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Explicit partition size in bytes. Mutually exclusive with -SizePercent.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Partition size expressed as a percentage of the target disk size. Mutually exclusive with -SizeBytes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Explicit partition size in bytes. Mutually exclusive with -SizePercent.
Partition size expressed as a percentage of the target disk size. Mutually exclusive with -SizeBytes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resizes the recovery partition on disk 0 partition 5 to 2 GiB.

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads or copies a recovery boot image to a local destination.

Downloads a WIM from an HTTPS source or copies it from a local or UNC path to the supplied destination. When DestinationPath names an existing directory or ends with a directory separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise it is treated as the target file path. Idempotent when the destination already matches the source.
Source file path on a local or UNC volume used as the input to the copy.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Image classification (WindowsRE, WindowsPE, BootWim) used when tagging the result.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill System Description }}
HTTPS URI from which the recovery image should be downloaded.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Image classification (WindowsRE, WindowsPE, BootWim) used when tagging the result.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Headers Description }}
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill System Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Image classification (WindowsRE, WindowsPE, BootWim) used when tagging the result.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Source file path on a local or UNC volume used as the input to the copy.
HTTPS URI from which the recovery image should be downloaded.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Headers Description }}
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill System Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Downloads the boot image from the supplied URI to C:\RecoveryImages\boot.wim.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates the metadata of an existing recovery partition.
Updates editable metadata such as the friendly label of an existing recovery partition.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
When set, marks the partition as hidden so it is omitted from common UI surfaces.
File system label assigned to the recovery volume. Defaults to RECOVERY.
When set, prevents Windows from automatically assigning a drive letter to the partition.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
When set, marks the partition as hidden so it is omitted from common UI surfaces.
File system label assigned to the recovery volume. Defaults to RECOVERY.
When set, prevents Windows from automatically assigning a drive letter to the partition.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Renames the recovery partition on disk 0 partition 5.

maml:description.maml:para: Replaces the boot image on an existing recovery boot entry.

Stages a new boot image over the WIM an existing ramdisk recovery boot entry currently boots from, so the entry immediately uses the new image. The entry's staged path and boot.sdi are preserved and the BCD store is not modified. -Name accepts wildcards and updates every matching entry. Fails for entries that are not staged-WIM ramdisk entries or whose recovery partition cannot be resolved.
New source WIM to stage over the entry's current boot image.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
BCD GUID identifier of the target boot entry.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
New source WIM to stage over the entry's current boot image.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Object received from the pipeline that the cmdlet should act on.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Friendly display name of the boot entry to update. Supports wildcards (for example Grace or Recovery*) and updates every match; a name with no wildcard characters matches case-insensitively.
New source WIM to stage over the entry's current boot image.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
New source WIM to stage over the entry's current boot image.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
BCD GUID identifier of the target boot entry.
Object received from the pipeline that the cmdlet should act on.
Friendly display name of the boot entry to update. Supports wildcards (for example Grace or Recovery*) and updates every match; a name with no wildcard characters matches case-insensitively.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Replaces the boot image on the named recovery entry with winre-new.wim.

maml:description.maml:para: Configures Windows RE / push-button reset as a recovery entry point.

Optionally registers a WindowsRE image, enables Windows RE, and schedules a boot into Windows RE on the next restart. Custom BCD boot entries are created by New-WindowsRecoveryBootEntry; this cmdlet no longer creates them, so boot-entry creation has a single, unambiguous path. -EntryPointMode BootEntry or Both is rejected and redirected to New-WindowsRecoveryBootEntry.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Selects which recovery entry points to configure: PushButton, BootEntry, or Both.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Friendly action keyword translated to a Windows recovery push-button reset action (for example Reset, Refresh, FactoryReset, BootToRE).
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Path to a WindowsRE WIM image that should be staged into the recovery partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Selects which recovery entry points to configure: PushButton, BootEntry, or Both.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Friendly action keyword translated to a Windows recovery push-button reset action (for example Reset, Refresh, FactoryReset, BootToRE).
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Path to a WindowsRE WIM image that should be staged into the recovery partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables Windows RE and schedules a boot into it on the next restart.
Splatted OrderedDictionary example for push-button / Windows RE configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Registers a WindowsRE image and/or schedules a boot to RE.
Registers a WindowsRE image with the operating system and optionally schedules a boot into Windows RE on the next restart.
When set, schedules the system to boot into the Windows Recovery Environment on the next restart.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Reagentc target identifier used when registering or scheduling a WindowsRE boot.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Path to a WindowsRE WIM image that should be staged into the recovery partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
When set, schedules the system to boot into the Windows Recovery Environment on the next restart.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Reagentc target identifier used when registering or scheduling a WindowsRE boot.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Path to a WindowsRE WIM image that should be staged into the recovery partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Registers the supplied WindowsRE image and returns the updated configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies or updates a Windows RE or Windows PE image.
Copies the supplied source image to a destination folder. The copy is skipped when the destination already matches the source by size and last-write timestamp.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Source WIM file that should be copied or staged.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill System Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Headers Description }}
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
HTTPS URI from which the recovery image should be downloaded.
{{ Fill System Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Suppresses interactive prompts and overrides safety refusals that would otherwise block destructive or risky changes.
Returns the resulting object after the operation completes. By default the cmdlet returns nothing on success.
Source WIM file that should be copied or staged.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Target file or directory path. When it names an existing directory or ends with a path separator the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the value is treated as the full destination file path and the source is renamed on copy.
{{ Fill Headers Description }}
{{ Fill Hidden Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
HTTPS URI from which the recovery image should be downloaded.
{{ Fill System Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Copies winre.wim to the recovery volume and returns the staged image record. When DestinationPath ends with a directory separator or names an existing directory, the source leaf name is appended; otherwise the destination is treated as a full file path and the source is renamed on copy.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests a recovery partition for size, layout, and contents.
Inspects a recovery partition and returns a structured pass/fail report covering size, layout, and presence of a WindowsRE image.
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Number of the physical disk to operate on, as reported by Get-Disk.
Number of the partition on the target disk, as reported by Get-Partition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Validates the recovery partition on disk 0 partition 5.
